site.name: CMSBar SvelteKit example
site.description: A site edited in place with CMSBar, server-rendered by SvelteKit with the framework-neutral CMS API mounted as one endpoint.

home.title: CMSBar runs in this SvelteKit host
home.description: A site server-rendered by SvelteKit, with the framework-neutral CMSBar API mounted as one catch-all endpoint.

hero.headline: Click anything. Change it. Ship it as a PR.
hero.subtitle: This text is editable - log in and click it.

demo.title: CMSBar runs in this SvelteKit host
demo.intro: This heading and paragraph are server-rendered by SvelteKit from the framework-neutral content model. The +page.server.ts load reads getContent() on the server and passes it to +page.svelte - so the content is in the HTML, not assembled by client JS. The whole CMSBar API is mounted at /api/cms/* through a single catch-all +server.ts that forwards the raw web Request to createCmsApi().
demo.body:
Rich text, edited in place

This block renders real HTML from the content model. Log in as an editor and a floating toolbar appears on selection: headings, lists, and links.

  • Bold, italic, underline
  • H2 / H3 / H4 block formats
  • Bulleted and numbered lists
